task_description: Find a specific song video, create and copy a short clip from it with a title, then open the copied clip link in a new browser tab.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X centered inside the long YouTube search input box, specifically over the blank text-entry area outlined in blue. In the full-screen view, Google Chrome is open on the YouTube homepage in Chinese, with the search bar expanded near the top center and a drop-down list of previous searches visible beneath it, including entries such as “taylor swift - love story,” “michael jackson - billie jean,” and others. The X is on the search field itself, which is a top-area UI control.
caption.think: The user intends to place the text cursor into the YouTube search field so they can enter or refine a search query.
caption.action: Click inside the YouTube search box.
caption.expectation: The search field becomes active with a text caret ready for typing, and the search suggestions remain visible or update based on focus.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in view centers on the YouTube search bar at the top of the page. The text entry field is active with the query visible as "taylor swift - love stor", showing that the phrase is being entered into the search box. A drop-down autocomplete panel is open directly beneath it with suggestions such as "taylor swift - love story" and related variants, indicating the typed text is refining the search rather than clicking a suggestion. In the full-screen view, Google Chrome is open on the YouTube homepage in Chinese, with the left navigation sidebar visible and video thumbnails in the main feed behind the suggestions panel.
caption.think: The user is typing the full song title into YouTube's search field to search for the Taylor Swift song "Love Story," likely completing or replacing the current partial query with the full intended phrase.
caption.action: Type "TAYLOR SWIFT - LOVE STORY" into the YouTube search box, completing the current partial text in the active field.
caption.expectation: The search box updates to the full query "taylor swift - love story," and the autocomplete suggestions refresh to match the completed song title.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows the YouTube search field with the completed query text "taylor swift - love story" inside it; a semitransparent red X appears over the lower part of the search area, but the raw action is a keyboard action. In the full-screen view, Google Chrome is open on YouTube in Chinese, and an autocomplete drop-down is expanded beneath the top search bar with suggestions such as "taylor swift - love story," "taylor swift - love story live," and lyric-related variants.
caption.think: The search phrase has already been entered, and the user now wants to submit the query rather than click a suggestion from the dropdown.
caption.action: Press ENTER to search for "taylor swift - love story".
caption.expectation: YouTube should load the search results page for "taylor swift - love story," replacing the suggestions list with matching videos.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The view is framed on the full YouTube results page with no red X, consistent with a scroll action. The page is already showing search results for "taylor swift - love story" in the top search bar. Visible results include a large thumbnail with the words "LOVE STORY" and the result title "Taylor Swift - Love Story (Lyrics)," with another result partially visible below. The right-side scrollbar thumb is near the top, indicating the page is close to the beginning of the results list.
caption.think: The user has submitted the YouTube search and now wants to move farther down the search results page to browse more videos beyond the currently visible top results.
caption.action: Scroll down on the YouTube results page.
caption.expectation: The results list should move upward, revealing additional videos lower in the search results.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X placed directly over the video result title text "Taylor Swift - Love Story." Beneath it are the view count and age in Chinese, and below that the channel row with the Taylor Swift name and verification checkmark. In the full-screen YouTube search results page, this title belongs to a standard search result card in the main results list, not a toolbar control or blank area. The page is showing results for "taylor swift - love story," and this clicked item is a video result object on the page.
caption.think: The user likely wants to open this specific official-looking video result for "Taylor Swift - Love Story" from the search results.
caption.action: Click the video result title "Taylor Swift - Love Story."
caption.expectation: That search result should be selected and its YouTube watch page should open, loading the video player for the chosen video.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X directly over the leftmost playback control in the YouTube video overlay, the round button with two vertical bars representing the Pause control. A small tooltip above it reads "暂停" with the keyboard hint "K." This is a UI control, not the video content itself. In the full-screen view, the YouTube watch page for "Taylor Swift - Love Story" is open in Chrome, the video is currently playing at the start, and the playback controls are visible along the bottom of the player.
caption.think: The user likely wants to stop the currently playing video momentarily by activating the player's pause button.
caption.action: Click the Pause button (the round control with two vertical bars) in the YouTube video player.
caption.expectation: The video should pause immediately, the motion in the player should stop, and the control should switch to a Play icon indicating playback is paused.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X centered on the rounded button labeled "剪辑," which has a scissors icon. A small tooltip above it also reads "剪辑," confirming it is the Clip control in the YouTube action bar. This is a UI control, not video content. In the full-screen view, the YouTube watch page for "Taylor Swift - Love Story" is open in Chrome, the video player is visible, and below it the row of action buttons includes Share, Save, the scissors-shaped Clip button, Download, and a more-options menu.
caption.think: The user likely wants to create or edit a clip from the current YouTube video by opening the Clip interface.
caption.action: Click the "剪辑" button with the scissors icon below the video.
caption.expectation: The Clip feature should open, likely showing a clip creation panel or dialog where a segment of the video can be selected and named.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X centered on the left time input box displaying "0:02.5" in the YouTube clip editor. This is a UI control in the side panel, specifically the clip start-time field, not video content or a blank area. In the full-screen view, Chrome is open on the YouTube watch page for "Taylor Swift - Love Story," and the right-side "创建剪辑" panel is open with title input, start and end time boxes, and a clip timeline; the clicked field is the start time box before the end time field showing "0:17.5."
caption.think: The user likely wants to edit or select the clip's starting timestamp so it can be adjusted for the desired video segment.
caption.action: Click the start time input box showing "0:02.5" in the clip editor panel.
caption.expectation: The start-time field should become active and selected, allowing the user to type a new time or adjust the clip start position.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in area is the right-hand time input box in YouTube's clip editor side panel. The field is active with a text cursor at the end, and it currently shows the end time being edited as "0:15." indicating the user is entering a timestamp into the clip end-time field rather than clicking a new control. In the full-screen view, Chrome is open on the YouTube page for "Taylor Swift - Love Story," and the "创建剪辑" panel is visible on the right with a title area, start time box showing "0:00.0," end time box being edited, and a clip timeline below.
caption.think: The user is setting the clip's end timestamp to 15 seconds so the clip spans from the beginning of the video to 0:15.0.
caption.action: Type "0.00:15.0" into the active end-time input box, replacing the current partial value.
caption.expectation: The end-time field should update to the newly entered 15-second timestamp, and the clip duration/timeline preview should adjust accordingly.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X positioned over a blank light-gray area directly above the time input boxes in the YouTube clip editor side panel. The nearby visible controls include the right time box containing "0:15.0" with a text cursor still inside it, but the X itself is not on the box or on any button, icon, handle, or text label. In the full-screen view, Chrome is on a YouTube watch page with the "创建剪辑" panel open on the right; the panel shows the channel name, title field, start and end time boxes, a clip thumbnail timeline, and action buttons below. This click is on a blank region within the side panel, not on a toolbar item, canvas object, or control point.
caption.think: The user likely wants to confirm the typed end time and remove focus from the active input field without changing tools or opening anything else.
caption.action: Click the blank light-gray area in the clip editor panel above the time input boxes.
caption.expectation: The active end-time field should lose text-edit focus while keeping the entered value, with no menu or dialog opening and no tool or mode changing.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The text entry area in the zoomed-in region is the clip title field near the top of YouTube's right-side "创建剪辑" panel, directly under the user name "liao young." The field currently contains the lowercase text "hell" with the text cursor at the end, indicating active text entry and that more characters are being appended rather than typing into the time boxes below. In the full-screen view, Google Chrome is open on a YouTube watch page for "Taylor Swift - Love Story," with the clip editor panel showing the title field, start and end time boxes set to "0:00.0" and "0:15.0," a short clip timeline preview, and the blue "分享剪辑" button.
caption.think: The user is finishing the clip title by adding the final character so the title becomes "hello."
caption.action: Type "HELLO" into the active clip title field, appending the needed text to complete the title content.
caption.expectation: The clip title field should update immediately to reflect the typed text, likely resulting in the word "hello," while the clip editor remains open and ready for sharing.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on the blue rounded button labeled “分享剪辑” at the bottom of the YouTube “创建剪辑” side panel. This is a UI control, not a blank area or canvas object. Nearby on the left is the text button “取消.” In the full-screen view, Chrome is open on a YouTube watch page for “Taylor Swift - Love Story,” the clip editor panel on the right shows the title “hello,” start time “0:00.0,” end time “0:15.0,” and a short clip timeline preview.
caption.think: The user has finished configuring the clip title and duration and now wants to share or generate the YouTube clip from the editor.
caption.action: Click the blue “分享剪辑” button.
caption.expectation: YouTube should process the clip and immediately open a sharing result, such as a share dialog, generated clip link, or the next sharing interface.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on the blue rounded button labeled “复制” at the right end of the share-link field inside YouTube’s sharing pop-up. This is a UI control button, not a blank area. Above it are share options such as WhatsApp, Facebook, X, email, and a right-arrow button. In the full-screen view, Google Chrome is on the YouTube page for “Taylor Swift - Love Story,” with a centered “分享” share dialog open over the video and the recommendations column visible on the right.
caption.think: After generating the clip share dialog, the user likely wants to copy the clip URL to the clipboard for pasting elsewhere.
caption.expectation: The clip link should be copied to the clipboard immediately, and the share dialog will likely remain open while briefly indicating the copy succeeded.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on the small tab close button—the dark "×" at the right side of the active Chrome tab titled "Taylor Swift - Love Story." This is a top-area browser UI control, not page content. In the full-screen view, Google Chrome is open to a YouTube page with a centered "分享" dialog still visible over the video, and the click target is on the browser tab itself rather than inside the YouTube dialog.
caption.think: The user likely wants to close the current YouTube browser tab now that the clip link has been copied.
caption.action: Click the tab close "×" button on the active Chrome tab.
caption.expectation: The current YouTube tab should close immediately, and Chrome should switch focus to the next remaining tab.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The focused area is the Chrome address bar at the top of the window. It is active with a blue outline and currently empty, showing the Google multicolor "G" icon on the left and the placeholder text indicating search or URL entry. No typed characters are visible yet. In the full-screen view, Google Chrome is on a new tab page in Chinese, with the large Google logo centered, a search box in the page body, several round shortcut icons below, and two tabs visible at the top: the left tab for "Taylor Swift - Love Story" and the active tab labeled "新标签页".
caption.think: The user likely copied the YouTube clip link in the previous steps, opened a new tab, and now wants to paste that copied URL into the address bar to visit it. Since this is a hotkey paste action, the content should replace the empty address field rather than append to existing text.
caption.action: Press Ctrl+V to paste the copied link into the active Chrome address bar.
caption.expectation: The copied URL should appear in the address bar immediately, ready to be opened if Enter is pressed next.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows the Chrome tab strip and the top of the address bar area. A semitransparent red X is visible over the active tab labeled “新标签页,” but the raw action is a keyboard action, not a click. In the full-screen view, Google Chrome is open on a new tab page with the Google logo centered. The address bar contains a pasted YouTube clip URL, and the same URL appears highlighted in the first suggestion row beneath it, indicating the browser is ready to navigate to that address.
caption.think: The user has already pasted the copied YouTube clip link into the address bar and now wants to open it. Pressing Enter should submit the URL and load the destination page.
caption.expectation: Chrome should navigate away from the new tab page and begin loading the YouTube clip URL in the current tab.
